explanation:
**Sports training** combines science (physiology, biomechanics) and practical experience. Training zones, progressive load, recovery, and nutrition are the pillars of running performance.

## How It's Calculated

This calculator applies the Riegel formula with your input values, checking typical ranges and alerting you to values outside the reasonable spectrum.

## Final Notes

This calculation is an orientative reference. For critical decisions (medical, training-related), consult with a specialized professional. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: What formula does this calculator use?
faq.a: The Riegel 1981 formula with an empirical exponent of 1.06. This accounts for the relationship between race distances in running performance prediction.

faq.q: How accurate is this 10K time prediction?
faq.a: Within ±2-5% for trained runners who have run both distances with similar preparation. Results are most accurate when you have recent 5K performance data.

faq.q: Can I use this if I'm at different training levels?
faq.a: This formula works best when your training level is similar for both distances. If you've been specifically training for 10K or 5K recently, the prediction may be less accurate.

faq.q: How do I estimate my marathon time?
faq.a: Use: t_marathon = t_10k × (42.2/10)^1.06. Note that marathon predictions are less precise than 10K projections from 5K.

faq.q: What are alternatives to this calculator?
faq.a: VO2max-based calculators, heart rate formulas, or training-specific pace calculators. Each has different accuracy depending on your fitness profile.

faq.q: Is this running calculator completely free?
faq.a: Yes, all our running calculators are free and require no registration or account.

faq.q: Are these results guaranteed to be accurate?
faq.a: No. These are orientative projections using internationally validated formulas. For important training decisions, consult a running coach or sports professional.

faq.q: Do you collect my running data?
faq.a: No. All calculations happen in your browser. We don't send your data to any server.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology

dataUpdate.notes: Formula is fixed and does not require external data updates
